name: Strategy Cascade & Audience Translation Ontology
alternateName: CASCADE-ONT — Audience Cascade, Translation Fidelity & Channel Mapping
description: Formalizes the audience cascade model for strategy communication. Strategy must be translated — not diluted — for each organisational audience. Defines five cascade levels (Board → C-Suite → Leadership → Teams → Individuals) with translation rules governing detail depth, language register, and metric granularity at each level. Fidelity assessments detect drift between intended and perceived strategy. Channel mappings connect the right medium to the right audience at the right cadence. Part of VE-Series VSOM-SC (Strategy Communication).
oaa:domain: Strategy Communication, Audience Management, Organisational Alignment

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Narrative-to-Audience Cascade
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Core SC cascade: a strategic narrative is translated for each audience level, with fidelity tracked between adjacent levels. An AI agent traverses from narrative through translations to each audience level, checking fidelity at each step.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: nar:StrategicNarrative → csc:translationOfNarrative → csc:CascadeTranslation → csc:translationForLevel → csc:CascadeLevel [Board → CSuite → Leadership → Teams → Individuals]
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Cascade strategy communication through organisational tiers with fidelity tracking

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Fidelity Drift Detection
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Compare fidelity scores between adjacent cascade levels to detect where strategy translation degrades. Enables targeted intervention at the level where drift occurs.
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Identify and correct cascade levels where strategic intent is being lost

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Cascade-to-OKR Connection
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Team and individual cascade levels connect directly to OKR objectives, making strategy personally relevant. Each person can trace their objectives back through the cascade to the board-level direction.
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Personal strategy connection — every individual's work traces to strategic direction

entities.name: AudienceCascade
entities.description: Container for a complete audience cascade model. Links a strategic narrative to a set of cascade levels, each with audience-appropriate translations, fidelity tracking, and channel mappings. An AudienceCascade is created for each StrategicNarrative and governs how that narrative flows through the organisation.

oaa:properties.name: narrativeRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to nar:StrategicNarrative @id being cascaded

oaa:properties.name: vsomFrameworkRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:VSOMFramework @id

oaa:properties.name: overallFidelityScore
oaa:properties.description: Aggregate fidelity score across all cascade levels (0-100)

oaa:properties.name: cascadeStatus
oaa:properties.description: Current state of the cascade deployment

oaa:properties.name: levelCount
oaa:properties.description: Number of active cascade levels (typically 5)

entities.name: CascadeLevel
entities.description: One of five organisational audience tiers in the strategy cascade. Each level represents a distinct audience with specific communication needs, language expectations, and metric granularity requirements. Levels map to RRR executive roles. The cascade flows Board → C-Suite → Leadership → Teams → Individuals, with each level receiving an audience-appropriate translation of the strategic narrative.

oaa:properties.name: levelType
oaa:properties.description: Audience tier in the cascade

oaa:properties.name: levelOrder
oaa:properties.description: Position in cascade (1=Board, 2=CSuite, 3=Leadership, 4=Teams, 5=Individuals)

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: What this audience tier needs from strategy communication

oaa:properties.name: languageRegister
oaa:properties.description: Expected language style (e.g. 'financial, governance, shareholder value' for Board)

oaa:properties.name: detailDepth
oaa:properties.description: Level of detail appropriate for this audience

oaa:properties.name: metricGranularity
oaa:properties.description: Metric resolution appropriate for this audience

oaa:properties.name: roleRefs
oaa:properties.description: References to rrr:ExecutiveRole @ids typical for this level

oaa:properties.name: typicalSize
oaa:properties.description: Typical audience size range (e.g. '5-12' for Board, '50-200' for Leadership)

entities.name: CascadeTranslation
entities.description: An audience-appropriate translation of a strategic narrative for a specific cascade level. Each translation preserves the strategic intent while adapting language, detail depth, and metric granularity for its target audience. AI agents generate draft translations; humans review and refine. The translation references specific narrative sections and adapts them per the level's TranslationRule.

oaa:properties.name: narrativeRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to nar:StrategicNarrative @id being translated

oaa:properties.name: levelRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to csc:CascadeLevel @id this translation targets

oaa:properties.name: translatedContent
oaa:properties.description: The audience-appropriate version of the narrative

oaa:properties.name: translationRuleRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to csc:TranslationRule @id governing this translation

oaa:properties.name: keyMessages
oaa:properties.description: Top 3-5 key messages for this audience

oaa:properties.name: metricHighlights
oaa:properties.description: Metrics relevant to this audience level

oaa:properties.name: callToAction
oaa:properties.description: What this audience should do differently as a result

oaa:properties.name: generatedBy
oaa:properties.description: Who generated this translation

entities.name: FidelityAssessment
entities.description: Fidelity score measuring how accurately strategy was translated between two adjacent cascade levels. A high fidelity score (>80) means the translation preserved strategic intent. A low score (<60) indicates drift — the audience at the lower level is receiving a materially different message than what was intended at the upper level. AI agents compute fidelity scores; humans validate and investigate root causes of drift.

oaa:properties.name: cascadeRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to csc:AudienceCascade @id

oaa:properties.name: upperLevelRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to csc:CascadeLevel @id (sender)

oaa:properties.name: lowerLevelRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to csc:CascadeLevel @id (receiver)

oaa:properties.name: fidelityScore
oaa:properties.description: Fidelity score (0-100). >80 = high, 60-80 = moderate, <60 = drift detected

oaa:properties.name: driftAreas
oaa:properties.description: Specific areas where translation drift was detected

oaa:properties.name: rootCause
oaa:properties.description: Root cause of fidelity gap if score < 80

oaa:properties.name: remediationAction
oaa:properties.description: Recommended action to correct drift

oaa:properties.name: assessedBy
oaa:properties.description: Who performed the assessment

entities.name: TranslationRule
entities.description: Defines the translation parameters for converting a strategic narrative to a specific audience level. Rules govern detail depth, language register, metric granularity, and what to include/exclude at each level. AI agents use translation rules to generate draft translations; the rules make the translation process systematic and auditable.

oaa:properties.name: levelRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to csc:CascadeLevel @id this rule applies to

oaa:properties.name: languageRegister
oaa:properties.description: Expected tone and vocabulary (e.g. 'financial governance' vs 'task-oriented')

oaa:properties.name: metricGranularity
oaa:properties.description: Level of metric detail to include

oaa:properties.name: includeElements
oaa:properties.description: Narrative elements to include for this audience

oaa:properties.name: excludeElements
oaa:properties.description: Narrative elements to exclude for this audience

oaa:properties.name: maxLength
oaa:properties.description: Recommended maximum length (e.g. '1 page', '5 minutes', '30 seconds')

oaa:properties.name: formatGuidance
oaa:properties.description: Preferred format (e.g. 'slide deck', 'email', 'town hall script', '1-on-1 talking points')

entities.name: ChannelMapping
entities.description: Maps the communication medium to a specific audience level at a specific cadence point. Different channels have different reach, depth, and engagement characteristics. Board members receive formal papers; teams receive sprint-context briefings; individuals receive 1-on-1 talking points. The mapping connects audience, cadence, and medium into a delivery plan.

oaa:properties.name: levelRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to csc:CascadeLevel @id

oaa:properties.name: channel
oaa:properties.description: Communication medium

oaa:properties.name: format
oaa:properties.description: Specific format within the channel (e.g. 'strategy-on-a-page PDF' for BoardPaper)

oaa:properties.name: owner
oaa:properties.description: Role responsible for delivering communication via this channel

oaa:properties.name: reachEstimate
oaa:properties.description: Expected reach as percentage of target audience

oaa:properties.name: engagementType
oaa:properties.description: Nature of engagement this channel supports

entities.name: CascadeMetric
entities.description: Measures the effectiveness of strategy cascade at a specific level. Three dimensions: reach (did the message get to the audience?), comprehension (did they understand it?), and alignment (are they acting on it?). These are lagging indicators — measured after communication delivery. Combined with fidelity scores (leading indicator), they give a complete picture of cascade health.

oaa:properties.name: levelRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to csc:CascadeLevel @id being measured

oaa:properties.name: cascadeRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to csc:AudienceCascade @id

oaa:properties.name: metricType
oaa:properties.description: Which cascade effectiveness dimension

oaa:properties.name: score
oaa:properties.description: Score (0-100)

oaa:properties.name: measurementMethod
oaa:properties.description: How this was measured (e.g. 'survey', 'OKR alignment check', 'attendance tracking')

oaa:properties.name: benchmark
oaa:properties.description: Previous score for trend comparison

relationships.name: Cascade For Narrative
relationships.description: Audience cascade is created for a specific strategic narrative

relationships.name: Has Level
relationships.description: Audience cascade contains cascade levels (typically all 5)

relationships.name: Cascades To Level
relationships.description: One cascade level flows to the next lower level

relationships.name: Translation Of Narrative
relationships.description: Cascade translation is an audience-specific version of a narrative

relationships.name: Translation For Level
relationships.description: Cascade translation targets a specific audience level

relationships.name: Cascade Level Maps To Role
relationships.description: Cascade level maps to RRR executive/functional roles at that organisational tier

relationships.name: Fidelity Between Levels
relationships.description: Fidelity assessment measures translation accuracy between two adjacent cascade levels

relationships.name: Cascade Connects To OKR
relationships.description: Team and individual cascade levels connect to OKR objectives for personal strategy relevance

relationships.name: Channel For Level
relationships.description: Channel mapping defines communication medium for a cascade level

relationships.name: Metric For Level
relationships.description: Cascade metric measures effectiveness at a specific level

businessRules.name: Cascade Must Reference Narrative
businessRules.action: THEN narrativeRef MUST reference a valid nar:StrategicNarrative @id

businessRules.name: Translation Must Have Call To Action
businessRules.action: THEN callToAction MUST be non-empty — every audience must know what to do differently

businessRules.name: Fidelity Must Be Between Adjacent Levels
businessRules.action: THEN upperLevelRef.levelOrder MUST be exactly one less than lowerLevelRef.levelOrder (adjacent levels only)

businessRules.name: Low Fidelity Requires Root Cause
businessRules.action: THEN rootCause and remediationAction MUST be specified

businessRules.name: Every Level Must Have Translation
businessRules.action: THEN each CascadeLevel in the cascade MUST have at least one CascadeTranslation with status 'Approved' or 'Delivered'

changeControl.changeNotes: Initial release of CASCADE-ONT as part of VE-Series VSOM-SC (Strategy Communication). Formalizes audience cascade model with five levels, translation rules, fidelity scoring, channel mapping, and effectiveness metrics. Cross-references NARRATIVE-ONT v1.0.0, VSOM-ONT v3.0.0, RRR-ONT v4.0.0, OKR-ONT v2.0.0, BSC-ONT v1.0.0, KPI-ONT v1.0.0.
